q1: Can you be my primary care doctor?
q1_a: Yes. Dr. Kanani offers primary care through a membership model known as Direct Primary Care, where you pay the practice a monthly fee for a direct, ongoing relationship.
q2_a: Dr. Kanani does not bill health insurance at this time, but all members are required to have insurance to cover lab testing, imaging, and hospital care. We can provide a superbill you can submit to your insurer for possible reimbursement.
q3: What is functional medicine, and how does it work?
q3_a: Functional medicine uses in-depth testing for hormones, gut health, and nutritional status to address the root causes of many chronic conditions. For functional medicine there's no membership fee — it's pay-as-you-go.
q4: How much testing will I need?
q4_a: Chronic conditions usually call for in-depth testing to uncover underlying causes; these specialized tests are typically in the range of a few hundred dollars. Standard laboratory testing is covered by your health plan.
q5: How often will you need to see me?
q5_a: Appointments are usually spaced six to eight weeks apart, depending on what you're working through.
q6: Will I have access to you between appointments?
q6_a: Yes. Email communication with Dr. Kanani is included in the cost of your appointments.
q7: How long does a treatment plan take to work?
q7_a: It depends on the condition. Some problems begin to improve within a few weeks; others take many months and require consistent work and effort.
q8: Do you coordinate care with my other doctors?
q8_a: Yes — we're glad to coordinate care with your specialists.
q9: Where are you located, and do you offer remote visits?
q9_a: The office is at 10024 Newtown Road, Nevada City, CA. Telehealth visits are available for many appointments — please ask when you reach out.
q10: What if I have an emergency, are you on call?
q10_a: No. Dr. Kanani is not available after hours for emergencies. If you have a medical emergency, call 911 or go to your nearest emergency room.
fq_sub: The practical details on membership, insurance, testing, and what working with Dr. Kanani looks like.
